qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Non-constant-time stateless reset token comparison enables timing side-channel attack
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding concerns the stateless reset token comparison in quinn-proto/src/connection/packet_crypto.rs using stateless_reset_token.as_deref() and byte-slice equality; the advisory concerns a panic in quinn-proto/src/transport_parameters.rs from unwrap() on malformed/truncated QUIC transport parameter varint decoding.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No manifest-listed qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause. The advisory is an unauthenticated remote DoS in quinn-proto transport parameter parsing, where malformed quic_transport_parameters cause fallible varint decoding to return UnexpectedEnd and then panic through unwrap(). The qualifying Quinn finding is a distinct stateless reset token timing side-channel in packet_crypto.rs.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-6xvm-j4wr-6v98 describes quinn-proto transport parameter parsing in transport_parameters.rs and an unwrap() panic on Err(UnexpectedEnd) from malformed/truncated varint encodings.

timing_side_channel_stateless_reset/vulnerability_report.json identifies packet_crypto.rs:41-42 stateless_reset_token.as_deref() == Some(...) as the root cause, bypassing ResetToken constant-time comparison.
